description: Single-source policy pack for governance, code rules, maintenance contracts, and generated maintenance docs.

governance.description: Unified governance section nested inside the single-source policy pack.

menu_simplification_manifest_rule.statement: Menus must stay simple; any feature common to 2D/3D/4D menus must live in the shared settings menu unless a very strong mode-specific reason is documented, with custom topology editing owned by the Explorer Playground.

banned_python_literals.message: settings_hub_model.py must not hardcode settings section header ownership; use config/menu/structure.json
settings_hub_model.py must not hardcode settings section filters; use config/menu/structure.json
settings_hub_model.py must not hardcode settings footer row ownership; use config/menu/structure.json
settings_hub_model.py must not hardcode top-level settings headers; use config/menu/structure.json

directives.statement: Start restructuring or behavior changes with a short plan and acceptance criteria.
Update relevant RDS docs plus BACKLOG and CURRENT_STATE when scope or behavior changes.
Run verify locally before completion; CI entrypoint remains authoritative.
Track LOC delta and prefer net LOC reduction for non-feature refactors.
Use quiet test/verify output by default; use verbose output only for diagnosis.
Prefer medium-sized localized patches over ultra-narrow patch fragmentation; split only when a broader patch becomes hard to review or tool-rejected.

Use apply_patch only for localized code edits with fresh exact context. For broad doc rewrites, generated maintenance files, or files already edited in the current batch, prefer one deterministic scripted rewrite immediately instead of patch-first retries. Patch-tool rejection is a method switch signal, not a blocker.

Do not use ad hoc multiline PowerShell -replace or BOM-producing Set-Content flows for source-file edits. Non-patch rewrites of source files must preserve UTF-8 without BOM and avoid literal escape-text insertion.
After any non-patch source rewrite, run a touched-file hygiene pass: encoding sanity, no literal escape artifacts, then focused lint before broader tests.
Never run verify.sh and ci_check.sh in parallel. Use focused checks first, then run the full gates sequentially.
When running pytest, verify, or CI wrapper commands locally, use the current repository virtualenv interpreter when one is available instead of an ambient system Python.
Do not retry apply_patch repeatedly on the same file. After one rejected apply_patch attempt on a file, switch immediately to the deterministic rewrite path for the rest of that edit.
For staged UI and architecture migrations, do not treat partial progress as completion. Do not mark complete until every stated acceptance criterion is explicitly satisfied.
Use additive migration first: add new modules, route one flow, verify, then remove old paths. Do not attempt broad destructive replacement in one step.

At the end of staged migration work, provide a delta report listing files added, files modified, files not touched, satisfied acceptance criteria, unsatisfied acceptance criteria, remaining old paths, and follow-up blockers.

code_rules.description: Unified code rule manifest for sanitation, magic numbers, config-backed runtime constants, wheel reuse, dead code, and LOC guidance.

authoritative_sources.description: repo-owned runtime constants, UI/layout defaults, animation timings, and cache limits
gameplay thresholds, scoring/tuning parameters, and other mutable runtime balance values

selected_file_bans.message: leaderboard max entries must come from config/project/constants.json via project_constant_int

rules.description: Prefer stdlib or existing repo helpers for parsing and validation rather than ad-hoc local variants.
Do not duplicate local sanitization and normalization pipelines where shared helpers exist.
Use existing project config, path, and JSON helper flows before introducing custom wrappers.
Prefer stdlib functional utilities over ad-hoc rewrites for common collection transforms.

maintenance_contract.title: tet4d canonical maintenance contract
maintenance_contract.description: Machine-checked source-of-truth rules for docs/tests/help/backlog/RDS/feature-map consistency.

must_contain: Authority files must be tracked in Git
prefer the current repo virtualenv interpreter when one is available
Prefer the current repo virtualenv for local pytest, verify, and CI-style runs whenever it is available.
Potential unsafe path traversal patterns detected in docs/ or config/.

Engine.description: small compatibility facade used mainly by replay and explicit compatibility tests
canonical kick candidate generation and shared resolution
explorer-only general gluing kernel, validation, mapping, and movement graph helpers

UI.description: bootstrap, pause/help, tutorial overlay, shared loop helpers
launcher, settings, bot, leaderboard flows, including `settings_hub_model.py`, `settings_hub_actions.py`, and `launcher_settings.py`

sources_of_truth.description: thin dispatch for contributor and agent entry
human workflow explainer for repo process and verification
active backlog and current change footprint

deprecated_authorities.description: Retired governance and routing surfaces that must stay absent from live authority docs and policy files.
